SEARCH
Package timgutilities.textio

Class ChoiceDescriptionData<P>

java.lang.Object
timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<P>
Type Parameters:
P - If using the contained ChoiceDescription(s) to hold an object (for example when listing directory entries) then P represents the object type they are holding
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cloneable
public class ChoiceDescriptionData<P> extends Object implements Cloneable
This class represents a set of choice inputs for the choice engine.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    ABANDON_TEXT
    the deault text to be used for abandon options

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ChoiceDescriptionData()
    Build an empty version, will need to be populated by adding options separately
    ChoiceDescriptionData(String[] options)
    build from an array with just options text
    ChoiceDescriptionData(String[] options, String[] descriptions)
    build based on array input for the choice text and description
    ChoiceDescriptionData(String[] options, String[] descriptions, String[] additionals)
    Built based on array input for all the descriptions, all arrays must be the same size
    ChoiceDescriptionData(Collection<String> options)
    build from a collection of jst the options text
    ChoiceDescriptionData(List<ChoiceDescription<P>> cds)
    build from a list of choice description objects that already exist

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addAbandonOption()
    If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected.
    void
    addAbandonOption(boolean abandonIsDefault)
    Creates an abandon option, you can chose if it will be the default or not If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected.
    void
    addAbandonOption(boolean addFirst, boolean abandonIsDefault)
    Creates an abandon option, you can chose if it will be the default or not If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected.
    void
    addAbandonOption(String abandonText)
    If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected.
    void
    addAbandonOption(String abandonText, boolean addFirst)
    Creates an abandon option and you can chose if it will at the begining or end of the list The text you provide will be used as the abandon text
    void
    addAbandonOption(String abandonText, boolean addFirst, boolean abandonIsDefault)
    Creates an abandon option and you can chose if it is to be used as the default The text you provide will be used as the abandon text If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected.
    void
    addChoiceDescription(Collection<ChoiceDescription<P>> cds)
    adds all of the choice description objects in the collection, can't be done once pre-selection processing has happened
    void
    addChoiceDescription(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
    Add a choicedesription object, cannot be done if the choices data has already been processed
    boolean
    choiceDescriptionIsPresent(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
    check if an option is in the list
    ChoiceDescriptionData<P>
    clone()
    Do a shallow clone, with the exception that the list of choice descriptions is a new list, but contains the original choice descriptions
    void
    completeAndLock()
    Triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only.
    String
    getAdditional(int i)
    present the options and get the users choice returning the additional text for that choice.
    String
    getChoice(int i)
    Present the options and get the users input returning the option text for that choice If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only.
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    getChoiceDescription(Integer index)
    Get the choice description for the specified index, if the index is null negative or more than the number of descriptions (this counts from zero) then null is returned.
    String
    getChoicesString()
    scan all of the options and get their choice strings, combine them and return If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only.
    String
    getData(int i)
    get the combined data (option, description and additional) for the option at that index, if it's the abandon option then only the text is returned.
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    getDefaultOption()
    gets the default option
    Integer
    getDefaultOptionNumber()
    gets the number of the default option
    String
    getDescription(int i)
    Present the options and get the users input returning the associated description for that choice.
    P
    getParam(int i)
    present the otpions and get the users choice returning the param object for that choice If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only.
    boolean
    isAbandoned(int i)
    checks if the option at index i is the abandon option
    int
    length()
    how many options are currently held, note that if the pre-selection processing has happened this may include an abandon option
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    locateChoiceDescriptionByOptionString(String option)
    Locate the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied string to the option name specified when creating the choice descriptions.
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    locateChoiceDescriptionByParam(P param)
    Locate the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied param to the param specified when creating the choice descriptions.
    Integer
    locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByChoiceDescription(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
    Locate the number of the choice description (if any) in the list, this used the equals method, so in principle if there are multiple descriptions that match only the first is returned.
    Integer
    locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByOptionString(String option)
    Locate the number of the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied string to the option name specified when creating the choice descriptions.
    Integer
    locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByParam(P param)
    Locate the number of the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied param to the param specified when creating the choice descriptions.
    boolean
    removeChoice(int i)
    remove the choice at the specified index, can't be done once pre-selection processing has happened
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    setDefaultByChoiceDescription(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
    sets the default choice using the provided choice description, and return the identified item.
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    setDefaultByIndex(Integer index)
    Sets the choice description at the specified index to be the default
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    setDefaultByOption(String option)
    sets the default choice using the provided option using the string equals, and return the identified item.
    ChoiceDescription<P>
    setDefaultByParam(P param)
    sets the default choice using the provided param using it's equals method, and return the identified item.
    void
    setDoSort(boolean doSort)
    If set to true then before the choices are presented the choices will be sorted by the option text
    void
    setSeparateFields(boolean separateFields)
    If true then when the text is displayed it will be splie between open, description and additional using "," If false they will just be concatenated
    String
    toString()
    generates the list of options as a string If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only.

  • Field Details

    • ABANDON_TEXT

      public static final String ABANDON_TEXT
      the deault text to be used for abandon options
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • ChoiceDescriptionData

      public ChoiceDescriptionData(String[] options, String[] descriptions, String[] additionals)
      Built based on array input for all the descriptions, all arrays must be the same size
      Parameters:
      options - an array of option strings (will be presented as the option)
      descriptions - an array of description string
      additionals - an array of additional info strings

    • ChoiceDescriptionData

      public ChoiceDescriptionData(String[] options, String[] descriptions)
      build based on array input for the choice text and description
      Parameters:
      options - an array of option strings (will be presented as the option)
      descriptions - an array of description string

    • ChoiceDescriptionData

      public ChoiceDescriptionData(String[] options)
      build from an array with just options text
      Parameters:
      options - an array of option strings (will be presented as the option)

    • ChoiceDescriptionData

      public ChoiceDescriptionData(Collection<String> options)
      build from a collection of jst the options text
      Parameters:
      options - a collection of option strings (will be presented as the option)

    • ChoiceDescriptionData

      public ChoiceDescriptionData(List<ChoiceDescription<P>> cds)
      build from a list of choice description objects that already exist
      Parameters:
      cds - ChoiceDescriptions to add

    • ChoiceDescriptionData

      public ChoiceDescriptionData()
      Build an empty version, will need to be populated by adding options separately

  • Method Details

    • clone

      public ChoiceDescriptionData<P> clone()
      Do a shallow clone, with the exception that the list of choice descriptions is a new list, but contains the original choice descriptions

    • setDoSort

      public void setDoSort(boolean doSort)
      If set to true then before the choices are presented the choices will be sorted by the option text
      Parameters:
      doSort - the doSort to set

    • setSeparateFields

      public void setSeparateFields(boolean separateFields)
      If true then when the text is displayed it will be splie between open, description and additional using "," If false they will just be concatenated
      Parameters:
      separateFields - the separateFields to set

    • addChoiceDescription

      public void addChoiceDescription(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
      Add a choicedesription object, cannot be done if the choices data has already been processed
      Parameters:
      cd - ChoiceDescription to add

    • addChoiceDescription

      public void addChoiceDescription(Collection<ChoiceDescription<P>> cds)
      adds all of the choice description objects in the collection, can't be done once pre-selection processing has happened
      Parameters:
      cds - ChoiceDescriptions to add

    • isAbandoned

      public boolean isAbandoned(int i)
      checks if the option at index i is the abandon option
      Parameters:
      i - index of the ChoiceDescription to check
      Returns:
      true if it is, ralse if not

    • removeChoice

      public boolean removeChoice(int i)
      remove the choice at the specified index, can't be done once pre-selection processing has happened
      Parameters:
      i - index of the ChoiceDescription to remove
      Returns:
      true if successful

    • getChoice

      public String getChoice(int i)
      Present the options and get the users input returning the option text for that choice If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Parameters:
      i - the option string of the option at that index
      Returns:
      the choice

    • getChoiceDescription

      public ChoiceDescription<P> getChoiceDescription(Integer index)
      Get the choice description for the specified index, if the index is null negative or more than the number of descriptions (this counts from zero) then null is returned.
      Parameters:
      index - the index to use to get the choice
      Returns:
      the choice

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription(int i)
      Present the options and get the users input returning the associated description for that choice. If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Parameters:
      i - the index of the ChoiceDescription whose description to get
      Returns:
      gets the description string for the chosen option

    • getAdditional

      public String getAdditional(int i)
      present the options and get the users choice returning the additional text for that choice. If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Parameters:
      i - number of option
      Returns:
      gets the additional string for the chosen option

    • getParam

      public P getParam(int i)
      present the otpions and get the users choice returning the param object for that choice If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Parameters:
      i - the index of the ChoiceDescription whose param to get
      Returns:
      the returned param

    • completeAndLock

      public void completeAndLock()
      Triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.

    • length

      public int length()
      how many options are currently held, note that if the pre-selection processing has happened this may include an abandon option
      Returns:
      the number of choices

    • getData

      public String getData(int i)
      get the combined data (option, description and additional) for the option at that index, if it's the abandon option then only the text is returned. If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Parameters:
      i - the index of the ChoiceDescription whose data to get
      Returns:
      the text data form the choice

    • toString

      public String toString()
      generates the list of options as a string If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • addAbandonOption

      public void addAbandonOption()
      If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected. otherwise you really should be using the param as the thing to look for, as otherwise your code may be unhappy it will cause the getParam(length()) to return null. The abandon option is not set to the default The abandon option will be added at the end of the list Default text will be added as the abandon option.

    • addAbandonOption

      public void addAbandonOption(boolean abandonIsDefault)
      Creates an abandon option, you can chose if it will be the default or not If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected. otherwise you really should be using the param as the thing to look for, as otherwise your code may be unhappy it will cause the getParam(length()) to return null. The abandon option will be added at the end of the list Default text will be added as the abandon option.
      Parameters:
      abandonIsDefault - if true the abandon option will be the default option (possibly overiding a previous one) if false the default won't be set

    • addAbandonOption

      public void addAbandonOption(boolean addFirst, boolean abandonIsDefault)
      Creates an abandon option, you can chose if it will be the default or not If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected. otherwise you really should be using the param as the thing to look for, as otherwise your code may be unhappy it will cause the getParam(length()) to return null. The abandon option will be added at the end of the list if addFirst is false or the beginning if it's true Default text will be added as the abandon option.
      Parameters:
      addFirst - if true the abandon option will be the first entry in the choices, if false it will be added at the end
      abandonIsDefault - if true the abandon option will be the default option (possibly overiding a previous one) if false the default won't be set

    • addAbandonOption

      public void addAbandonOption(String abandonText, boolean addFirst)
      Creates an abandon option and you can chose if it will at the begining or end of the list The text you provide will be used as the abandon text
      Parameters:
      abandonText - the text to use for the abandon option
      addFirst - if true the abandon option will be the first entry in the choices, if false it will be added at the end

    • addAbandonOption

      public void addAbandonOption(String abandonText)
      If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected. otherwise you really should be using the param as the thing to look for, as otherwise your code may be unhappy it will cause the getParam(length()) to return null. The text you provide will be used as the abandon text The abandon option will be added at the end of the list The abandon option is not set to the default
      Parameters:
      abandonText - the text to use for the abandon option

    • addAbandonOption

      public void addAbandonOption(String abandonText, boolean addFirst, boolean abandonIsDefault)
      Creates an abandon option and you can chose if it is to be used as the default The text you provide will be used as the abandon text If an abandon choice has been added then you can use isAbandoned(choice number) to see if it was the abandoned that was selected. otherwise you really should be using the param as the thing to look for, as otherwise your code may be unhappy it will cause the getParam(length()) to return null If addFirst is true then the abandon option will be at the start of the options, if not it will be later Only one abandon options is allowed and doing so "freezes" the data structure, so you can't add additional entries. If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Parameters:
      abandonText - the text to use for the abandon option
      addFirst - where to add the abandon option
      abandonIsDefault - if the abandon option is the default choice

    • getDefaultOption

      public ChoiceDescription<P> getDefaultOption()
      gets the default option
      Returns:
      the default option (if one has been set) null if not

    • getDefaultOptionNumber

      public Integer getDefaultOptionNumber()
      gets the number of the default option
      Returns:
      the index of the default option (is set) null if not

    • setDefaultByChoiceDescription

      public ChoiceDescription<P> setDefaultByChoiceDescription(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
      sets the default choice using the provided choice description, and return the identified item. If no matching choice is located then return null to indicate this. If there was an existing choice then this will override this, and if there is and existing choice but no choice description matching the call argument is found the existing option will be set to null
      Parameters:
      cd - the default option to set
      Returns:
      the default option

    • setDefaultByIndex

      public ChoiceDescription<P> setDefaultByIndex(Integer index)
      Sets the choice description at the specified index to be the default
      Parameters:
      index - the index of the option to use as the default
      Returns:
      the option that was set

    • setDefaultByOption

      public ChoiceDescription<P> setDefaultByOption(String option)
      sets the default choice using the provided option using the string equals, and return the identified item. If no matching choice is located then return null to indicate this. If there was an existing choice then this will override this, and if there is and existing choice but no choice description matching the call argument is found the existing option will be set to null
      Parameters:
      option - the name of the option to set as the default
      Returns:
      the option that was set as the default

    • setDefaultByParam

      public ChoiceDescription<P> setDefaultByParam(P param)
      sets the default choice using the provided param using it's equals method, and return the identified item. If no matching choice is located then return null to indicate this. If there was an existing choice then this will override this, and if there is and existing choice but no choice description matching the call argument is found the existing option will be set to null
      Parameters:
      param - the param to use to locate the option to make the default
      Returns:
      the option that was set as the default

    • choiceDescriptionIsPresent

      public boolean choiceDescriptionIsPresent(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
      check if an option is in the list
      Parameters:
      cd - the option to check
      Returns:
      true if it is present, false if not

    • locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByChoiceDescription

      public Integer locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByChoiceDescription(ChoiceDescription<P> cd)
      Locate the number of the choice description (if any) in the list, this used the equals method, so in principle if there are multiple descriptions that match only the first is returned.
      Parameters:
      cd - the option to look for
      Returns:
      the index of the option (or null if not present)

    • locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByOptionString

      public Integer locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByOptionString(String option)
      Locate the number of the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied string to the option name specified when creating the choice descriptions. This used the String equals method. If there are multiple matches with the same option this will ONLY find the first match.
      Parameters:
      option - the option string to check for
      Returns:
      the index of the option or null if not present

    • locateChoiceDescriptionByOptionString

      public ChoiceDescription<P> locateChoiceDescriptionByOptionString(String option)
      Locate the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied string to the option name specified when creating the choice descriptions. This used the String equals method. If there are multiple matches with the same option this will ONLY find the first match.
      Parameters:
      option - the option string of the option to check for
      Returns:
      the option if present or null if not

    • locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByParam

      public Integer locateChoiceDescriptionIndexByParam(P param)
      Locate the number of the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied param to the param specified when creating the choice descriptions. This used the param's equals method. If there are multiple matches with the same option this will ONLY find the first match.
      Parameters:
      param - the param to be used to check for the option *
      Returns:
      the index of the option if present or null if not

    • locateChoiceDescriptionByParam

      public ChoiceDescription<P> locateChoiceDescriptionByParam(P param)
      Locate the choice description (if any) in the list buy comparing the supplied param to the param specified when creating the choice descriptions. This used the param's equals method. If there are multiple matches with the same option this will ONLY find the first match.
      Parameters:
      param - the param to be used to locate the option
      Returns:
      the chosen option if present or null if not

    • getChoicesString

      public String getChoicesString()
      scan all of the options and get their choice strings, combine them and return If not already done triggers the process of handling sort, adding abandons etc, and makes the list read only. Only call when you're ready to use this.
      Returns:
      a string representing the choices for display
